user.title: Sonos Conductor
user.description: Presence-aware multi-room volume orchestration for Sonos. Pick the speakers the conductor should manage — you will configure one zone per speaker in the next steps.

data_description.speakers: Sonos media players found in your entity registry. Music Assistant mirrors and non-Sonos players are excluded automatically.

zone.title: Zone {zone_number} of {zone_count}: {speaker}
zone.description: Each speaker gets one zone: the presence inputs that make it audible, and how it blends with the rest of the house. The suggestions below come from the speaker's area.

data_description.name: Display name for the zone. It also becomes the zone's internal ID, so it must be unique.
data_description.room: Zones that share air get the same room name (for example, an open living room and its dining nook both say "stue"). Speakers in one room are scaled by 1/√N so the perceived total loudness stays constant. Same text = same room.
data_description.presence_entity: A Presence Conductor room-occupancy sensor — the preferred presence source. Beyond on/off it reports activity (passing/active/settled), which scales the hold time: a walk-through releases quickly, a settled room lingers. Auto-suggested when a room matches this speaker's area.
data_description.occupancy: Plain motion, occupancy or presence sensors — the fallback when no Presence Conductor room exists (extra sensors can be combined with one). While any input is on, this zone is audible.
data_description.tvs: TV players attached to this zone. While a TV plays, the zone counts as occupied and its room scale is forced to 1.0 so a TV remote gets a 1:1 volume mapping.
data_description.hold_seconds: How long occupancy may be absent before the zone fades out. Raise it for rooms you briefly step out of, like a kitchen.
data_description.fallback: The fallback zone stays audible when no other zone is active, so the music never dies while someone is home. At most one zone can be the fallback.
data_description.trim: Loudness ratio compensating for hardware differences (for example Move 1.2, Era 100 1.1, Arc 1.0). A speaker's target volume is master × trim × room scale.
data_description.dock_sensor: Battery-charging sensor that reports when this portable speaker is docked. While undocked, the speaker runs standalone and the conductor leaves it alone. Leave empty for non-portable speakers.

ducks.title: Duck inputs
ducks.description: Binary sensors (doors, windows, gates) that temporarily cap all playing speakers while active — for example, dropping the music while the entrance door is open.

data_description.duck_entities: While any of these sensors is on, every playing speaker is capped at the duck volume.
data_description.duck_volume: Absolute volume cap while ducked. If several duck inputs are active, the lowest cap wins.
data_description.release_fade: Seconds to fade back to normal volume after the last duck input clears.

home.title: Home presence
home.description: Optionally gate the fallback zone on home-level presence. While this sensor reports that nobody is home, the fallback zone stays silent instead of playing to an empty home — and fades back in when someone returns. Leave empty to keep the fallback always on.

data_description.home_presence_entity: A home-level presence sensor, e.g. Presence Conductor's "Anyone home". If it becomes unavailable, the conductor fails safe and behaves as if someone is home.

tunables.title: Tunables
tunables.description: Fine-tuning for fades and synchronization. The defaults mirror the behavior of a battle-tested installation; you can revisit them anytime via the integration's Configure button.

data_description.fade_in: Seconds to fade a zone in when it becomes active.
data_description.fade_out: Seconds to fade a zone out after its hold time expires.
data_description.rebalance_fade: Fade used when already-audible speakers are rebalanced (room scale or reverse sync changes).
data_description.master_fade: Fade for master-volume fan-out. Keep at 0 to track a volume slider tightly.
data_description.sync_threshold: Minimum implied master change before an external volume change is synced back to the master.
data_description.external_debounce: Seconds an external volume report must stay stable before it is trusted (absorbs slider drags in the Sonos app).
data_description.transition_suppression: Ignore external volume reports within this window after any zone, duck or TV transition — the fleet is in motion.
data_description.group_repair_delay: How long the speaker group topology may deviate before it is repaired.
data_description.startup_tolerance: Volume divergence below which startup adopts the current volumes as-is instead of converging them.
data_description.night_volume_cap: Absolute volume ceiling for every speaker while night mode is active. Duck inputs with a lower cap still win.
data_description.hold_passing_scale: Hold-time multiplier when a zone's visit never rose above passing (needs a Presence Conductor room). 0.3 means a walk-through fades out after 30% of the normal hold time.
data_description.hold_settled_scale: Hold-time multiplier after someone was settled in the zone (needs a Presence Conductor room). 4.0 means stepping out of a settled room keeps the music 4× the normal hold time.

error.invalid_zone_name: The zone name must contain at least one letter or digit.
error.duplicate_zone_name: Another zone already uses this name. Zone names must be unique.
error.multiple_fallback_zones: Only one zone can be the fallback, and another zone already is. Disable it there first.

abort.already_configured: Sonos Conductor is already set up. Only a single instance is supported — use Configure on the existing entry instead.
abort.no_speakers_found: No Sonos media players were found in the entity registry. Set up the Sonos integration first, then add Sonos Conductor.

init.title: Sonos Conductor options
init.description: Pick a section to edit. Changes apply when the section is saved; adding or removing speakers requires re-adding the integration.

menu_options.zones: Zones — presence, rooms, hold times, trim
menu_options.home: Home presence — silence the fallback when nobody is home

zones.title: Zone {zone_number} of {zone_count}: {zone}
zones.description: Adjust this zone's inputs and behavior. Current settings are prefilled; discovery only fills the gaps.

data_description.room: Zones that share air get the same room name. Speakers in one room are scaled by 1/√N so the perceived total loudness stays constant. Same text = same room.
data_description.presence_entity: A Presence Conductor room-occupancy sensor — the preferred presence source. Its activity (passing/active/settled) scales the hold time.
data_description.occupancy: Plain motion, occupancy or presence sensors — the fallback when no Presence Conductor room exists (extra sensors can be combined with one). While any input is on, this zone is audible.
data_description.tvs: TV players attached to this zone. While a TV plays, the zone counts as occupied and its room scale is forced to 1.0 so a TV remote gets a 1:1 volume mapping.
data_description.hold_seconds: How long occupancy may be absent before the zone fades out.
data_description.fallback: The fallback zone stays audible when no other zone is active. At most one zone can be the fallback.
data_description.trim: Loudness ratio compensating for hardware differences. A speaker's target volume is master × trim × room scale.
data_description.dock_sensor: Battery-charging sensor that reports when this portable speaker is docked. Leave empty for non-portable speakers.

ducks.title: Duck inputs
ducks.description: Binary sensors that temporarily cap all playing speakers while active. Currently configured sensors are preselected; deselect to remove them.

data_description.duck_entities: While any of these sensors is on, every playing speaker is capped at the duck volume.
data_description.duck_volume: Absolute volume cap while ducked. If several duck inputs are active, the lowest cap wins.
data_description.release_fade: Seconds to fade back to normal volume after the last duck input clears.

home.title: Home presence
home.description: Optionally gate the fallback zone on home-level presence. While this sensor reports that nobody is home, the fallback zone stays silent instead of playing to an empty home — and fades back in when someone returns. Leave empty to keep the fallback always on.

data_description.home_presence_entity: A home-level presence sensor, e.g. Presence Conductor's "Anyone home". If it becomes unavailable, the conductor fails safe and behaves as if someone is home.

tunables.title: Tunables
tunables.description: Fine-tuning for fades and synchronization. Current values are prefilled.

data_description.fade_in: Seconds to fade a zone in when it becomes active.
data_description.fade_out: Seconds to fade a zone out after its hold time expires.
data_description.rebalance_fade: Fade used when already-audible speakers are rebalanced (room scale or reverse sync changes).
data_description.master_fade: Fade for master-volume fan-out. Keep at 0 to track a volume slider tightly.
data_description.sync_threshold: Minimum implied master change before an external volume change is synced back to the master.
data_description.external_debounce: Seconds an external volume report must stay stable before it is trusted (absorbs slider drags in the Sonos app).
data_description.transition_suppression: Ignore external volume reports within this window after any zone, duck or TV transition — the fleet is in motion.
data_description.group_repair_delay: How long the speaker group topology may deviate before it is repaired.
data_description.startup_tolerance: Volume divergence below which startup adopts the current volumes as-is instead of converging them.
data_description.night_volume_cap: Absolute volume ceiling for every speaker while night mode is active. Duck inputs with a lower cap still win.
data_description.hold_passing_scale: Hold-time multiplier when a zone's visit never rose above passing (needs a Presence Conductor room). 0.3 means a walk-through fades out after 30% of the normal hold time.
data_description.hold_settled_scale: Hold-time multiplier after someone was settled in the zone (needs a Presence Conductor room). 4.0 means stepping out of a settled room keeps the music 4× the normal hold time.

media.title: Media & HomeKit sources
media.description: Choose which of the group leader's sources (Sonos favorites, radio stations, hardware inputs) the master player exposes. These appear as selectable inputs in HomeKit and on dashboards. Leave empty to expose all of them.

data_description.homekit_sources: Empty = mirror everything the leader offers. Reload the HomeKit bridge after changing this so the Home app picks up the new inputs.

error.multiple_fallback_zones: Only one zone can be the fallback, and another zone already is. Disable it there first.

abort.no_zones_configured: There are no zones to edit. Remove and re-add the integration to select speakers.
